1 hour ago

The recent Xbox layoffs have sparked a lot of discussion about Obsidian Entertainment, but one of the studio's directors says many people are getting the story completely wrong.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

⭐ Key Highlights
- Obsidian director Brandon Adler criticized people spreading misinformation about the studio.
- He said many online opinions come from people with "zero insight" into how the studio works.
- Adler stressed that many of the same developers behind classics like Fallout: New Vegas, The Outer Worlds, and Pillars of Eternity are still leading projects today.
- Obsidian was one of the studios affected by Microsoft's recent Xbox layoffs.
- Despite the changes, Adler says the studio's creative "DNA" remains the same.
- Reports also claim Avowed 2 has been canceled, while Obsidian has shifted its focus to a new Fallout game led by Josh Sawyer.
Adler admitted it has been an extremely difficult week for the team after the layoffs, but he was especially frustrated by people claiming that Obsidian is no longer the studio it once was.
According to him, many of the current directors and leads are the very same people who worked on some of the studio's most beloved games. While he agrees the company has changed over the past 20 years, he believes the core identity and passion behind Obsidian are still intact.
He also reminded fans that game development is a team effort and warned against trusting people who confidently speak about the studio without actually knowing how games are made or who worked on them.
For now, Obsidian appears to be moving forward despite the layoffs. While reports suggest Avowed 2 has been shelved, the studio is also rumored to be developing a brand-new Fallout game, giving fans something exciting to look forward to.
